title: chenopodium ambrosioides subsp. chilense
intro: Chenopodium ambrosioides subsp. chilense, also known as Mexican tea, is a plant species belonging to the family Amaranthaceae, and is native to South America. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, and is traditionally used to relieve symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory issues. The health benefits of Mexican tea include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate coughs, and soothe digestive issues, making it a popular herbal remedy. Mexican tea can be prepared as a tea by infusing its leaves in hot water, or by using its essential oils in aromatherapy preparations.
